act: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Achterhooks.
afar: adverb. At, to, or from a great distance; far away.
aft: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Afitti.
air: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Airoran.
aircraft: noun. A vehicle capable of atmospheric flight due to interaction with the air, such as buoyancy or lift.
ait: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Arikem.
arc: symbol. ISO 639-2 & ISO 639-3 language code for Aramaic.
arf: symbol. Former ISO 639-3 language code for Arafundi.
aria: noun. A musical piece written typically for a solo voice with orchestral accompaniment in an opera or cantata.
art: symbol. ISO 639-2 & ISO 639-5 language code for artificial languages.
car: symbol. ISO 639-2 & ISO 639-3 language code for Kari'na.
carat: noun. A metric unit of mass equal to exactly 200 mg, chiefly used for measuring precious stones and pearls.
carr: noun. Any marsh; marshy ground, swampland.
cart: noun. A small, open, wheeled vehicle, drawn or pushed by a person or animal, often with two wheels on one axle, more often used for
cat: symbol. ISO 639-2 & ISO 639-3 language code for Catalan.
fact: noun. Something actual as opposed to invented.
fair: adjective. Beautiful, of a pleasing appearance, with a pure and fresh quality.
far: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Fataleka.
fart: verb. To emit digestive gases from the anus; to flatulate.
fat: symbol. ISO 639-2 & ISO 639-3 language code for Fante Akan.
fiar: noun. One in whom the property of an estate is vested, subject to the estate of a liferenter.
fiat: noun. An arbitrary or authoritative command or order to do something; an effectual decree.
fir: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Firan.
fit: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Meänkieli.
frat: noun. Shortened form of fraternity, college organization.
friar: noun. A member of a mendicant Christian order such as the Augustinians, Carmelites (white friars), Franciscans (grey friars) or the
frit: noun. A pre- fused ceramic mixture used to make glass and glazes.
raft: noun. A flat - bottomed craft able to float and drift on water, used for transport or as a waterborne platform.
raia: noun. Alternative form of raya (“type of historical territory”).
rat: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Razajerdi.
ria: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Reang.
rif: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Tarifit.
tar: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Central Tarahumara.
tiara: noun. The three- tiered papal crown.
tic: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Tira.
triac: noun. A three- terminal electronic component that conducts current in either direction when triggered; a bidirectional triode thyristor.
